verdicts.evidence: _do_close() is still wrapped in asyncio.wait_for(timeout=5.0) (line 838), while the work it awaits stays synchronous: process_cleanup.kill_browser_process (line 722), self._stop_browser (731), and the TimeoutError fallback's kill/finalize/cleanup_deferred trio (850-852) are all called directly with zero asyncio.to_thread anywhere in the file (grep for to_thread returns nothing), so wait_for still cannot preempt a hung kill and the fallback re-runs the same unbounded synchronous calls. Only cosmetic drift from gates: asyncio.TimeoutError -> TimeoutError alias and ruff double-quotes.

The sole liveness signal is unchanged: _server_is_healthy still only does socket.create_connection/close. It remains the signal for every consumer named in the finding - reuse/status decisions at lines 132 and 211, the _clear_stale_backend eviction guard at 253, and the _watch_backend_liveness watchdog default at 391 - none of which verify the event loop / FastMCP dispatch is servicing requests. The application-level probe _await_backend_http exists but is called only once (line 465, startup). A wedged-but-listening backend still reports healthy on all three paths. Only gates change: except (socket.error, OSError) -> except OSError (socket.error is an OSError alias).

verdicts.new_quote:
@section_tool("debugging")
async def get_debug_view(
    max_errors: int = 50,
    max_warnings: int = 50,
    max_info: int = 50,
    include_all: bool = False,
verdicts.evidence: All three diagnostic readers are still ordinary @section_tool("debugging") MCP tool functions dispatched through the same request path as spawn_browser/navigate. No out-of-band transport was added: grep across server.py finds no flush, no SIGUSR/SIGBREAK handler, no healthz endpoint, and no periodic background task that drains DebugLogger buffers to disk. The F-303/F-304 fix (independent background flush task or secondary control port) has not executed, so when tool calls hang, the debug view hangs with them.

verdicts.new_quote:
check = is_healthy if is_healthy is not None else (lambda: _server_is_healthy(port))
    nap = sleep if sleep is not None else anyio.sleep
    consecutive = 0
    while True:
        await nap(interval)
        if check():
            consecutive = 0
            continue
        consecutive += 1
        if consecutive >= failures_before_teardown:
            return
verdicts.evidence: _watch_backend_liveness still defaults its check to the bare-TCP _server_is_healthy(port) (line 391), resets the failure counter on any successful connect (397), and only returns after 3 consecutive CONNECTION failures (400). The stronger real-initialize->HTTP-200 probe _await_backend_http (line 317, whose docstring still warns a freshly bound uvicorn socket answers before the MCP session manager is ready) is invoked only at startup (line 465), never in the watchdog. A wedged-but-listening backend passes forever and the proxy never tears down - the exact liveness gap. No Stage-3 liveness fix executed.

sys.path.append(str(project_root))

class ComprehensiveElementCloner:
verdicts.evidence: All 5 cloner modules still exist (element_cloner 836, file_based_element_cloner 750, comprehensive_element_cloner 406, cdp_element_cloner 389, progressive_element_cloner 310 LOC). comprehensive_element_cloner.py and cdp_element_cloner.py still import only debug_logger (no element_cloner import in either) and independently reimplement extraction, while file_based_element_cloner still cleanly delegates. No consolidation and no deletion happened. Only gates change: ruff dropped the unused 'import os' and collapsed 'from typing import Dict, Any, Optional' to 'from typing import Any'.

verdicts.new_quote:
"""Main MCP server for browser automation."""

import asyncio
import base64
import hashlib
import importlib
import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import sys
verdicts.evidence: server.py is still one monolithic module and has grown to 4421 lines (up from 4207 at baseline), still containing the debugging tools verified above plus config resolution and orchestration for every embedded/ subsystem. No section-based split occurred. Only gates change: isort reordered the header block (the baseline quote's 'import signal' moved out of the first eleven lines; 'import tempfile' now follows 'import sys').

self._lock is still a plain threading.Lock() (line 41), not RLock. export_to_file_paginated still acquires it via style (b) acquire(timeout=5.0) at 391 and then, while holding it, calls self.get_debug_view_paginated (405), whose body re-enters the same non-reentrant lock via bare 'with self._lock:' at 198. The inner acquire has no timeout, so same-thread reentrancy deadlocks on every call - the finding's exact mechanism, intact. Neither Stage-3 fix (single lock discipline or switch to RLock) executed; only the 'format' param was renamed 'fmt'.
